recent_excerpts: A new malware campaign deploys both Gh0st RAT and CloverPlus adware via a single obfuscated loader, giving attackers persistent remote control and a revenue stream from a single infection.
MiningDropper, a multi-stage Android malware framework, delivers infostealers, RATs, and banking trojans to devices via disguised apps, according to CyberSecurity News researchers.
A trojanized Slack installer delivers a remote access tool that creates a hidden desktop, allowing attackers to bypass multi-factor authentication and silently control victim machines.
